nodes.body: <<FILL role=setup words=122 beats='Marrow Hollow on the night of the Turning, the one night a year the old Cinderwick Exchange wakes; twelve-year-old Wren Ashby, chosen this year as the town's single new keykeeper, climbs the frost-bright lane to the tall brick message-and-clock house above the mills; as Wren reaches the door the whole building begins to hum and tick to life, dials spinning behind fogged glass; the stakes: at midnight the Exchange must send the true-time signal that keeps every clock, mill wheel, and school bell in the valley honest for the coming year, and tonight the signal is in trouble'>>

choices.label: Push open the great brass door and go in.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=116 beats='under the lobby's enormous dial, Nightmaster Odell Vane, spry and inkstained, greets Wren and explains the keykeeper's charge: each keeper minds one hall of the Exchange through the Turning night and keeps its part of the machine ready for the midnight signal; tonight, Vane says gravely, something has gone wrong in every one of the four halls at once, and he cannot mind them all; he presses a cold brass keyring into Wren's hand and says the choosing of a hall is the keeper's own, made once and kept till dawn'>>

choices.label: Ask Vane about the four halls.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=134 beats='Vane names the four halls and their trouble this Turning: the Clockloft, where the great pendulum drags and midnight will come late unless the mechanism is mended; the Whispering Gallery, where a false echo loops through the sound-dome and the true message must be found before the wrong one is sent; the Cistern, the flooded underworks whose water pressure drives the whole Exchange, now leaking toward a burst; and the Cartway, the pneumatic tube room, where the midnight capsule carrying the true-time token has jammed somewhere in the brass veins; the halls will not share a keeper, Vane says, and the night is already turning'>>

choices.label: Keep the Clockloft and mend the pendulum.
Keep the Whispering Gallery and find the true message.
Keep the Cistern and hold back the flood.
Keep the Cartway and free the midnight capsule.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=116 beats='the spiral stair to the Clockloft, cold and loud with brass; Junia, the retired clocksmith who tends the loft, waits by the great pendulum, which swings a shade too slow; she tells Wren the escapement is worn and midnight will fall late unless they win back the lost minutes before the hour'>>

choices.label: Follow Junia to the mechanism.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=114 beats='Junia opens the movement's glass and shows Wren the trouble: a worn pallet on the escapement, a count-wheel with a loose pin, and a pendulum bob whose trim has crept; any one of the three, mended right, could steady the tick in time'>>

choices.label: Study the three faults with Junia.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=112 beats='Wren reads the loft's frost-fogged gauges while Junia lays out her tools; the slow tick knocks in the dark like a tired heart, and the hour hand crawls toward a midnight that will not wait for a clumsy repair'>>

choices.label: Decide where to begin.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=130 beats='Junia says a keeper mends one fault well rather than three badly; the worn pallet can be re-cut at the bench, the jammed count-wheel freed by climbing the frame, or the dragging pendulum set right by rebalancing its heavy bob'>>

choices.label: Re-cut the worn escapement pallet at the bench.
Climb the frame to free the jammed count-wheel.
Rebalance the pendulum bob with trim-weights.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=114 beats='at the bench Wren files a new bearing face onto the steel pallet under Junia's eye, slow strokes, checking the fit against the escape wheel again and again until the shape comes true beneath the lamp'>>

choices.label: Ready the finished pallet.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=126 beats='the pallet is cut but soft; Junia offers two roads: harden it properly in the quench and lose precious minutes, or fit it green and gamble that soft steel holds through the single stroke of midnight'>>

choices.label: Temper it in the quench, the slow sure way.
Fit it green and race the clock.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=112 beats='Wren heats and quenches the pallet, counting breaths against the falling hour; the steel hisses, hardens, and comes out keen; Junia fits it while the loft holds its breath'>>

choices.label: Set the hardened pallet and watch the tick.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=102 beats='the escapement catches the new pallet, releases, catches; the slow knock quickens, evens, and climbs back to a true, bright tick; Junia's hands go still and the hour hand steadies exactly on its mark'>>

choices.label: Ring in the mended midnight.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=success words=96 beats='at midnight the Clockloft strikes clean and on time, and Wren feels the whole valley's clocks lean into the true signal; Junia says the loft has not ticked so honestly in years, and tells Wren a keeper who mends slow keeps well'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=110 beats='Wren fits the green pallet and the tick steadies, but Junia frowns at the soft new steel; for an hour it holds beautifully, then, minutes before midnight, the pallet burrs and the tick begins to limp again'>>

choices.label: Nurse the failing pallet toward midnight.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=setback words=96 beats='Wren and Junia hand-ease the movement through the hour and the Exchange sends its signal a few minutes late; the valley's clocks will run a touch behind all year, a small honest error; Junia says green steel and haste make their own kind of time, and next Turning they will do it slow'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=114 beats='Wren climbs into the loft's iron frame toward the jammed count-wheel, boots on cold girders, the pendulum sweeping past close enough to stir the air'>>

choices.label: Reach the stuck count-wheel.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=112 beats='clinging to the frame, Wren finds the fault: a dropped brass pin has wedged between two teeth, and behind it, scratched into the metal, are the initials of keykeepers going back a hundred Turnings'>>

choices.label: Work the wedged pin loose.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=112 beats='with a pick and a steady hand Wren eases the pin free; the count-wheel shivers and remembers how to turn, and Junia calls up that the tick is gathering strength'>>

choices.label: Climb down and read the old marks.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=102 beats='safe on the boards again, Wren finds a keeper's logbook Junia had never shown, each mended Turning recorded in a different hand; the count-wheel runs true and midnight rings clean while Wren reads a century of keepers by lamplight'>>

choices.label: Add a name to the log.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=discovery words=96 beats='midnight strikes on time, and Junia lets Wren scratch a new set of initials beside the old ones in the frame; the trouble was only a fallen pin, but Wren leaves the loft knowing the Exchange is really a chain of hands, each keeping the time for the next, and now Wren's hand is one of them'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=114 beats='Wren takes the pendulum road, sorting Junia's brass trim-weights by the gram, each meant to nudge the heavy bob a hair faster without throwing off its long, patient swing'>>

choices.label: Begin trimming the bob.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=112 beats='weight by weight the sweep quickens toward true; Junia teaches Wren to read the swing by ear, and warns that past a certain point more weight makes the bob wild instead of fast'>>

choices.label: Approach the true swing.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=126 beats='the tick sits almost perfect; Junia says trust the arithmetic and lock the weights now, or chase the last hair of error and add one more trim past her warning'>>

choices.label: Trust the math and lock the weights.
Chase perfection with one more weight.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=112 beats='Wren locks the trim collars exactly where the numbers say and steps back; Junia counts the swing under her breath and nods, once'>>

choices.label: Watch the pendulum find its stride.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=102 beats='the great bob settles into a long, even, unhurried sweep, and the tick rides it home; the hour hand walks up to its mark and stands there, patient and exact, as the loft warms with the sound of a clock that knows itself'>>

choices.label: Send the midnight signal.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=completion words=96 beats='at midnight the Clockloft leads the valley true, and Vane climbs up to mark in the great register that the Turning was kept; Junia tells Wren that knowing when to stop is the whole craft, and that the loft has found itself a keeper it can trust with time'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=110 beats='Wren adds the extra weight past Junia's warning; the bob lunges, the swing goes long and heavy and wrong, and a trim collar shakes loose and drops into the gears with a shriek'>>

choices.label: Grab for the runaway weight.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=capture words=96 beats='Wren's sleeve snags on the plunging bob and Junia hauls Wren clear by the collar just in time; both are safe, but the seized movement cannot be freed before the hour, and the Exchange stays silent at midnight; the valley will set its clocks by guess this year, and Junia holds Wren a long, shaken moment before she speaks'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=116 beats='the Whispering Gallery is a great dark sound-dome hung with brass horns; Doran, the gallery's half-deaf echo-reader, meets Wren with a lamp; a false echo is looping somewhere in the dome, he says, and if the wrong message rides the midnight signal the whole valley will keep a lie all year'>>

choices.label: Follow Doran into the sound-dome.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=114 beats='Doran explains the gallery's craft: real messages arrive as clean echoes read off the focus-stone in the dome's center, but tonight a garbled voice repeats and repeats, hiding the true one; three ways to run it down, he says, and only one night to try'>>

choices.label: Learn the three ways to catch the true echo.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=112 beats='Wren stands on the focus-stone while whispers slide down the dome from every side, some true, some false, all overlapping; picking the real message from the loop will take a cool head and a careful ear'>>

choices.label: Choose a method.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=130 beats='Doran lays out the choices: trace the false echo to its source horn on the gallery floor, sit the focus-stone and sift the true words out by patient listening, or climb the high catwalk to the master horn where every echo is born'>>

choices.label: Trace the false echo to its source horn.
Sit the focus-stone and sift the words by ear.
Climb the catwalk to the master horn.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=114 beats='Wren walks the gallery floor, ear to one brass horn after another, following the false voice as it grows louder toward one shadowed alcove'>>

choices.label: Close in on the loud horn.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=126 beats='the false echo pours from a cracked horn stuffed with an old felt baffle; Doran signs two choices: pull the baffle and silence the horn outright, or leave it and re-aim the horn so its lie folds back into itself'>>

choices.label: Re-aim the horn to cancel the false echo.
Pull the baffle and silence the horn.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=112 beats='Wren swings the heavy horn on its pivot by tiny degrees, Doran reading the dome with a hand flat on the stone, until the false voice meets its own returning wave'>>

choices.label: Find the cancelling angle.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=102 beats='at the exact angle the lie erases itself, and out of the sudden quiet rises the true message, clear and whole: the valley's time, spoken plain; Doran laughs a rusty laugh and copies it down'>>

choices.label: Carry the true message to the sender.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=completion words=96 beats='the true message rides the midnight signal and the valley keeps a whole honest year; Vane records the Turning kept, and Doran tells Wren that anyone can make noise but a keeper learns to unmake it, and that the gallery would be glad of such ears again'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=110 beats='Wren yanks the felt baffle and the false horn dies, but so does part of the dome's balance; the true echo is there now, but thin and hard to hold, and Doran must guess at two blurred words'>>

choices.label: Reconstruct the blurred message with Doran.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=discovery words=96 beats='between them they piece out most of the message and send it, guessing the last of it right by luck and sense; the valley keeps good time, and Doran teaches Wren that silencing a lie is easy but recovering the truth underneath is the real work, a lesson Wren turns over all the way down the stairs'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=114 beats='Wren sits cross-legged on the cold focus-stone and closes both eyes, letting the whole dome pour its whispers down at once instead of chasing any single one'>>

choices.label: Let every echo arrive together.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=112 beats='slowly the loop reveals its shape: the false voice always lags a half-beat and never breathes, while the true message, faint beneath it, keeps a living rhythm; Wren learns to hear the difference'>>

choices.label: Lock onto the breathing voice.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=112 beats='holding the true rhythm like a thread, Wren follows it word by word out of the tangle while Doran writes down each one Wren calls; the false voice rages louder but cannot break the thread'>>

choices.label: Call the last of the message.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=102 beats='the true message stands complete on Doran's slate, every word earned by listening; Doran seals it for the sender, and the false echo, unheeded at last, fades to nothing in the dome above'>>

choices.label: Send the listened message.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=success words=96 beats='the honest message goes out at midnight and the valley runs true; Doran tells Wren that most keepers try to shout the lie down, and that Wren instead learned to listen it apart, which is the rarer gift; he presses a small brass ear-horn into Wren's hand to remember the night'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=114 beats='Wren climbs the iron catwalk that rings the dome toward the master horn, the false echo growing colder and louder with every rung, the gallery floor dropping away below into dark'>>

choices.label: Climb toward the master horn.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=112 beats='on the high walk the false voice is almost a person, wheedling, telling Wren the safe rail is rusted and the true horn lies out past it on the old unsafe gantry, just there, only a few steps beyond the barrier'>>

choices.label: Weigh the coaxing voice.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=126 beats='Doran's warning shout comes thin from below: stay inside the rail; the false echo insists the true horn is just past the barrier on the old gantry; Wren must choose whom to trust, the friend below or the voice in the dark'>>

choices.label: Trust Doran and stay inside the rail.
Trust the voice and cross onto the old gantry.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=112 beats='Wren keeps a hand on the safe rail and ignores the coaxing; from inside the barrier the master horn is plainly reachable, and the false voice was only trying to lure a climber out over the drop'>>

choices.label: Reach the master horn safely.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=102 beats='at the master horn Wren finds the loose valve feeding the loop and closes it; the false voice snuffs out, and the whole dome fills with the single true message it had been drowning all night'>>

choices.label: Read the freed true message.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=discovery words=96 beats='the recovered message rides the midnight signal and the valley keeps true; climbing down, Wren understands the false echo was the gallery testing whether a keeper could tell a coaxing lie from a friend's warning, and Doran says Wren passed the only test that ever mattered up there'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=110 beats='Wren swings a leg over the barrier onto the old gantry, chasing the voice; the rusted iron groans, the promised horn is not there at all, only more dark, and the walkway tilts underfoot with a long tearing sound'>>

choices.label: Grab for the rail as the gantry gives.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=capture words=96 beats='the tilting gantry pins Wren against the cold rail, boots slipping over nothing, the promised horn never there at all; the false voice goes gleeful and then, cheated of a fall, sullen, while Wren clings in the swallowing dark, too turned around to find the way back; only Doran's lamp and steady called-out directions bring Wren inching off the ruined walkway to safety, long minutes gone; the gallery keeps its oldest, saddest lesson, that a voice which flatters you toward the edge was never a friend, and the Exchange sends no true message from the dome this Turning'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=116 beats='down slick stone stairs to the Cistern, the flooded underworks whose water pressure drives the whole Exchange; Marlow the cistern-keeper wades knee-deep with a storm-lantern; a seam is leaking, she says, and if the pressure drops before midnight the signal upstairs dies for want of breath'>>

choices.label: Wade in after Marlow.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=114 beats='Marlow shows Wren the works: the great header tank, the pressure gauge creeping down, and a hairline leak somewhere along the old brick main; fix the pressure in time, she says, and the Exchange breathes; fail, and midnight goes quiet'>>

choices.label: Look over the failing works with Marlow.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=112 beats='cold water tugs at Wren's boots while the gauge needle sinks; Marlow counts three ways to save the pressure before the hour, each with its own risk in the dark and the rising damp'>>

choices.label: Pick a way to hold the pressure.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=130 beats='Marlow lays it out: dive to find and plug the leaking seam at the main, work the hand-pumps to refill the header tank faster than it drains, or shut the great stop-valve to seal off the leaking section entirely'>>

choices.label: Dive to find and plug the leaking seam.
Work the hand-pumps to refill the tank.
Shut the great stop-valve on the leaking section.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=114 beats='Wren takes a breath and goes under along the brick main, feeling for the pull of escaping water, Marlow's lantern a wobbling green coin above the surface'>>

choices.label: Feel along the main for the leak.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=126 beats='Wren finds the seam, a finger-width crack sucking cold; two choices surface with the next breath: pack it fast with Marlow's lead wool, or take longer to set a proper clamp that will hold far better if the hour allows'>>

choices.label: Set the proper clamp, the strong slow fix.
Pack it fast with lead wool.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=112 beats='diving again and again, Wren fits the iron clamp across the seam and draws it tight bolt by bolt, lungs burning, Marlow timing each dive from above and hauling Wren up to breathe between'>>

choices.label: Draw the last bolt tight.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=102 beats='the clamp bites and the sucking stops; the gauge needle shivers, halts, and climbs; the great tank stops draining and the Exchange draws a deep, even breath through the pipes overhead'>>

choices.label: Watch the pressure climb home.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=success words=96 beats='at midnight the Cistern holds full pressure and the signal goes out strong; Marlow wraps Wren in a dry coat and says most folk fear going under the cold water, and that a keeper who faces it and fixes it right belongs down here whenever the valley needs one'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=110 beats='Wren jams the lead wool into the crack and the worst of the leak stops, but the fast patch weeps a slow thread of water that Marlow eyes with doubt as the gauge creeps only halfway back'>>

choices.label: Nurse the half-pressure toward midnight.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=setback words=96 beats='the patch holds just enough; the Exchange sends its signal thin but real, and the valley keeps time on a shallow breath this year; Marlow tells Wren a fast plug beats a burst main any night, but that next Turning they will set the clamp and do it properly, and Wren means to be there'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=114 beats='Wren throws off a coat and takes the long iron handle of the header pump, and Marlow takes the other; together they begin the brutal rhythm of hauling water up faster than the leak can steal it'>>

choices.label: Set the pumping rhythm with Marlow.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=112 beats='the tank rises a hand's width, then holds; Wren's arms turn to rope, but Marlow calls the stroke and the two of them find a pace they can just barely keep against the sinking gauge'>>

choices.label: Hold the pace against the clock.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=112 beats='as midnight nears the leak slows on its own, silt drawn into the crack, and the pumps at last gain on it; the gauge needle lifts steadily under Wren's aching, streaming effort'>>

choices.label: Pump the tank the last of the way full.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=102 beats='the header tank brims and overflows its mark; the Exchange breathes deep and even, and Marlow finally lets the handle rest, grinning through the sweat at a job done by nothing but two backs and a lot of will'>>

choices.label: Let the full tank feed the signal.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=completion words=96 beats='the Cistern holds and the midnight signal rings full and true; Vane comes down to record the Turning kept, and Marlow tells him the new keeper out-pumped a leak with bare arms; some nights, she says to Wren, the Exchange runs on muscle and stubbornness, and Wren has both'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=114 beats='Wren wades toward the great stop-valve, a rusted wheel as tall as a door, that will seal the whole leaking section away from the rest of the works if it can be turned'>>

choices.label: Reach the great stop-valve.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=112 beats='the wheel is seized with rust and years; Marlow warns that shutting it saves the pressure but strands the far pumps, so it must be turned fully or not at all, no half measures in the cold dark'>>

choices.label: Set both hands to the frozen wheel.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=126 beats='the valve gives an inch and stops; Marlow says brace it slow and steady and it will come, or throw all of Wren's weight against it at once and risk snapping the old spindle for good'>>

choices.label: Brace it slow and steady with Marlow.
Throw all your weight against it at once.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=112 beats='Wren and Marlow lean into the wheel together, a hand's turn at a time, letting the old iron remember its threads instead of forcing them'>>

choices.label: Walk the wheel around, turn by turn.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=102 beats='with a groan and then a rush the valve seats home; the leaking section falls silent and the pressure everywhere else surges back; the gauge climbs past its mark and steadies, and the Exchange breathes easy'>>

choices.label: Confirm the pressure holds.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=discovery words=96 beats='the sealed works hold pressure to midnight and the signal goes out true; reading the old valve's maker-mark by lantern, Wren learns the Cistern was built by the same hands that raised the Clockloft, one machine breathing through the whole hill, and Marlow says a keeper who sees the whole works has seen the real Exchange'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=110 beats='Wren throws everything into the wheel at once; it lurches a quarter-turn and the old spindle shears with a crack, jamming the valve half-open, neither sealed nor free, water pushing through the gap'>>

choices.label: Scramble back from the failing valve.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=setback words=96 beats='with the valve jammed half-shut the pressure cannot hold, and the Exchange sends no signal from the underworks this Turning; Wren and Marlow are cold and safe but beaten, and the valley will keep rough time this year; forcing the old iron, Marlow says quietly, only ever breaks it, a lesson Wren carries up the wet stairs'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=116 beats='the Cartway is a warm brass-veined room where message capsules whistle through pneumatic tubes; Effie, the young tube-runner, meets Wren wide-eyed; the midnight capsule carrying the true-time token has jammed somewhere in the veins, she says, and without it the sender has nothing true to send'>>

choices.label: Join Effie at the tube board.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=114 beats='Effie shows Wren the great routing board, a wall of brass tubes and levers mapping the whole Exchange; the midnight capsule is stuck somewhere between here and the sending station, and three routes might reach it before the hour'>>

choices.label: Read the routing board with Effie.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=112 beats='Wren traces the cold dead tube where the capsule went silent; Effie counts the ways to reach it, each running through a different stretch of the old brass maze, each with its own hazard'>>

choices.label: Choose a route to the jammed capsule.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=130 beats='Effie lays out the routes: crawl the service duct to the jam and clear it by hand, reroute the air pressure to blow the capsule free from the board, or send a chaser capsule down the tube to knock the stuck one loose'>>

choices.label: Crawl the service duct and clear the jam by hand.
Reroute the air pressure to blow it free.
Send a chaser capsule to knock it loose.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=114 beats='Wren wriggles into the tight brass service duct with a hand-lamp, following the dead tube by touch toward the muffled place where the capsule sits wedged'>>

choices.label: Crawl to the wedged capsule.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=126 beats='Wren reaches the jam: the capsule is crumpled against a sticky junction flap; two choices in the cramped dark, ease the flap open gently and free the capsule whole, or shove hard and trust speed over care'>>

choices.label: Ease the flap and free the capsule whole.
Shove it through hard and fast.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=112 beats='Wren works the stiff junction flap open a careful degree at a time until the dented capsule slides loose into waiting fingers, its true-time token safe and dry inside'>>

choices.label: Cradle the freed capsule and back out.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=102 beats='out of the duct, Wren loads the rescued capsule into the sending tube; Effie throws the lever and it whistles away clean toward the station, the token intact and the minutes just barely enough'>>

choices.label: Send the capsule to the station.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=completion words=96 beats='the true-time token reaches the sender and the midnight signal goes out whole; Vane marks the Turning kept, and Effie tells Wren that runners learn the veins with their hands in the dark, and that Wren has the touch for it; the Cartway would keep Wren gladly'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=110 beats='Wren shoves the capsule hard through the flap; it tears free but the dented case springs open, and the true-time token skitters loose into the dark bottom of the duct'>>

choices.label: Grope after the loose token.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=capture words=96 beats='Wren wedges deeper after the token and gets stuck fast in the narrowing brass, and Effie has to fetch Vane and a grease-can to work Wren gently out; everyone is safe, but the token is lost below and the Exchange sends no signal this Turning; haste in a tight place, Effie says, only ever wedges you tighter, and she stays close while they free Wren'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=114 beats='Wren stays at the board to reroute the air, and Effie coaches the trick: close every branch but the jammed one so the whole Exchange's pressure gathers behind the stuck capsule at once'>>

choices.label: Close the branch valves one by one.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=112 beats='lever by lever the board goes quiet as tubes fall shut, the gauges climbing as pressure banks up behind the jam; Effie watches the dial and tells Wren when to hold'>>

choices.label: Bank the pressure behind the jam.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=112 beats='at the right instant Wren throws the release; a great breath of air punches down the dead tube, and far off in the brass something knocks, shudders, and begins to move'>>

choices.label: Track the freed capsule down the board.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=102 beats='the midnight capsule comes whistling home to the board, shaken loose and whole; Effie catches it, whoops, and slots it straight into the sending tube while the last minutes drain away'>>

choices.label: Send the recovered capsule.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=success words=96 beats='the capsule reaches the sender and the signal goes out on time; Effie tells Vane that Wren cleared the whole vein without ever leaving the board, just by understanding how the air wanted to move; a keeper who thinks the machine through, Vane says, is worth three who only climb into it, and he shakes Wren's hand'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=114 beats='Wren loads a heavy chaser capsule to send down the tube after the jammed one, meaning to knock it loose the quick and simple way, and Effie sets the pressure for the shot'>>

choices.label: Ready the chaser capsule.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=112 beats='Effie warns that a chaser is a blunt tool: sent right it taps the jam free, sent too hard it drives both capsules into a worse wreck deeper in the veins where no hand can reach'>>

choices.label: Set the chaser's force.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=126 beats='the tube is loaded and Wren's hand is on the lever; Effie says send it soft and true, a gentle tap that might need a second try, or send it hard and settle the whole thing in one shot if it works'>>

choices.label: Send the chaser soft and true.
Send the chaser hard, one shot to settle it.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=112 beats='Wren feathers the lever and the chaser slips away with a soft whoosh; it coasts down the tube and meets the jam with the lightest of taps, exactly as Effie hoped'>>

choices.label: Wait on the gentle tap.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=102 beats='the tap is enough; the midnight capsule shakes loose and both capsules come coasting back to the board unhurt, the true-time token rattling safe inside; Effie grabs it and races it to the sending tube'>>

choices.label: Send the loosened capsule home.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=discovery words=96 beats='the signal goes out true at midnight; sorting the two returned capsules, Wren finds the chaser was an old training round from Effie's own first Turning, her initials scratched inside, and Effie goes quiet and glad; the Cartway remembers its runners, she says, and now it will remember Wren too'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=110 beats='Wren slams the lever full; the heavy chaser rockets down the tube and hits the jam with a bang that shakes the whole board, and both capsules crumple together deep in an unreachable bend of the brass'>>

choices.label: Search the board for any sign of the capsules.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=setback words=96 beats='the wrecked capsules sit jammed where no runner can reach before dawn, and the Exchange sends nothing from the Cartway this Turning; Wren and Effie are unhurt but low, and the valley will guess at its clocks this year; a chaser sent in anger, Effie says, only ever makes two jams out of one, and Wren nods, learning it the hard way'>>
